title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a fiery crash near Bedford, NH, after his Lucid Gravity hit a toll plaza.
Mirman, known for 'Bob's Burgers', was pulled from the car by a state trooper and security detail members.
He thanked his rescuers and hospital staff via Instagram, sharing his gratitude and humor after the accident.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, was charged after attacking a NH State Trooper in an unprovoked incident in Dover.
The assault occurred in a parking lot off Dover Point Road while the trooper was working in his cruiser.
Newcomb faces multiple charges including felony criminal threatening and attempting to take an officer's firearm.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, is running the Boston Marathon to support Mass General in memory of his late second mother.
Sharla Sieve raised over $123,000 for hepatic arterial infusions at MGH before passing from metastatic colorectal cancer.
This is Mallett's fourth marathon; he aims to honor Sharla's legacy by running Boston for the second consecutive year.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the New England Patriots to acquire Eagles' wide receiver A.J. Brown by June.
A.J. Brown's trade from the Eagles would lower his dead cap hit significantly after June 1.
The Patriots remain interested in A.J. Brown to strengthen their incomplete wide receiver roster.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is streaming on Netflix, featuring Alfie Williams and Ralph Fiennes.
Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee', available on Hulu, depicting the life of the Quaker leader.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' streams on PBS, featuring Jeff Goldblum and narrated by George Clooney.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Willson Contreras and Marcelo Mayer hit home runs as the Red Sox defeated the Padres 5-2 at Fenway Park.
Caleb Durbin broke a hitless streak with an RBI single, contributing to the Red Sox's first home win.
Red Sox pitcher Sonny Gray bounced back, allowing two runs over six innings in the victory against San Diego.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots will start phase one of their offseason program on April 20, focusing on conditioning and meetings.
The Patriots' phase three Organized Team Activities (OTAs) are scheduled for May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
A mandatory minicamp for the Patriots is set for June 15-17, followed by a training camp in July.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's latest mock draft predicts Patriots selecting edge rushers Cashius Howell and Gabe Jacas in the first two rounds.
Cashius Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season at Texas A&M; Gabe Jacas is noted for his strong run defense.
Patriots coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes drafting best available players rather than prioritizing specific team needs.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox won 5-2 against the Padres in their home opener at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, while Willson Contreras hit a home run.
Marcelo Mayer and Caleb Durbin contributed offensively, breaking previous performance slumps.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover is seeking grant funding to implement a body-worn camera program for its police department.
The initiative follows an incident where Officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was shot by a colleague, highlighting the lack of body cameras.
Since 2021, Massachusetts has provided nearly $13 million in grants for police body camera programs across 144 departments.
